rationale: I split Physics & Astronomy into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (covering the major communities authors self-identify with) and granularity (no single card >35% and no niche card <0.5%). I kept large, distinct communities like Condensed Matter, High Energy/Particle, Astrophysics and Cosmology separate because they differ in methods, literature and audiences, while combining strongly overlapping areas (e.g., Optics & Photonics; Geophysics & Planetary Physics) to avoid overly thin categories. I deliberately did not further split Condensed Matter into many narrow materials subfields because materials-focused work should route to Materials Science at level-1; likewise chemical-physics work is routed to Chemistry. Quantum Information is separated from AMO and Condensed Matter because it is a distinct, recognizable profession despite methodological overlap.

subspecialties.name: Condensed Matter
subspecialties.tagline: Solid-state, electronic, topological and correlated-matter physics
covers: Electron correlation, superconductivity, magnetism, topological phases
Examples: ARPES studies of correlated oxides; theory of topological insulators
explicitly_excludes: Materials Science (materials-focused experimental or engineering work)

subspecialties.name: High Energy Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Particle physics, collider experiments and theory
covers: Collider phenomenology, beyond-Standard-Model theory, particle detectors
Examples: LHC searches, effective field theory, neutrino oscillation analyses
explicitly_excludes: Nuclear Physics (low-energy nuclear structure and reactions)

subspecialties.name: Atomic, Molecular & Optical (AMO)
subspecialties.tagline: Atomic physics, molecular dynamics, laser-matter interactions
covers: Precision spectroscopy, cold atoms, ultrafast lasers, molecular collisions
Examples: atomic clocks, cold-atom experiments, molecular beam studies
explicitly_excludes: Quantum Information (when focus is on information protocols rather than AMO experiments)
Chemistry (molecular synthesis and chemical reaction mechanism work)

subspecialties.name: Quantum Information
subspecialties.tagline: Quantum computing, communication and information theory
covers: Quantum algorithms, error correction, hardware implementations and benchmarks
Examples: superconducting qubit experiments, photonic quantum networks, algorithm theory
explicitly_excludes: Condensed Matter (materials-focused qubit development should still map by discipline)

subspecialties.name: Astrophysics
subspecialties.tagline: Star, galaxy and high-energy astrophysical phenomena
covers: Observational and theoretical studies of stars, galaxies, transients and high-energy sources
Examples: supernova observations, galaxy formation simulations, X-ray binaries
explicitly_excludes: Cosmology (large-scale Universe, early-Universe theory and CMB studies)

subspecialties.name: Cosmology
subspecialties.tagline: Early Universe, large-scale structure and cosmological theory
covers: Inflation, dark matter/energy phenomenology, CMB and large-scale structure analyses
Examples: parameter constraints from surveys, theoretical models of inflation
explicitly_excludes: Astrophysics (galaxy- or star-focused astrophysical processes not about cosmology)

subspecialties.name: Plasma Physics & Fusion
subspecialties.tagline: Laboratory and space plasmas; magnetic and inertial fusion
covers: Magnetohydrodynamics, turbulence, fusion confinement, space plasma observations
Examples: tokamak confinement studies, solar wind plasma measurements
explicitly_excludes: Geophysics & Planetary Physics (planetary atmosphere dynamics routed there when appropriate)

subspecialties.name: Nuclear Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Nuclear structure, reactions and low-energy nuclear theory
covers: Properties of nuclei, nuclear astrophysics, heavy-ion collisions at low-to-intermediate energy
Examples: ab initio nuclear structure calculations, nuclear reaction experiments
explicitly_excludes: High Energy Physics (particle physics at collider energies)

subspecialties.name: Mathematical & Statistical Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Theory-focused: exactly solvable models, statistical mechanics, non-equilibrium physics
covers: Rigorous results, field theory methods, stochastic processes and critical phenomena
Examples: integrable models, KPZ universality, renormalization-group proofs
explicitly_excludes: Soft Matter & Complex Systems (applied complex-systems work with experiments may go there)

subspecialties.name: Optics & Photonics
subspecialties.tagline: Classical and quantum optics, lasers, photonic devices and applications
covers: Nonlinear optics, photonic materials, integrated photonics and imaging physics
Examples: frequency combs, nonlinear waveguide experiments, imaging methods
explicitly_excludes: AMO (fundamental atomic/molecular experiments focused on atoms/molecules rather than photonic devices)

subspecialties.name: Geophysics & Planetary Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Physical processes inside planets and planetary environments
covers: Seismology as physics, planetary interiors, magnetospheres and atmospheric physics of planets
Examples: mantle convection modelling, planetary magnetosphere observations
explicitly_excludes: Earth Sciences (applied geoscience that is primarily geology or resources-focused)

subspecialties.name: Instrumentation & Methods
subspecialties.tagline: Experimental techniques, detectors, measurement and data methods
covers: Detector development, measurement techniques, data pipelines and calibration methods
Examples: new CCD/bolometer designs, calibration methods for telescopes, analysis pipelines
explicitly_excludes: Engineering (purely applied engineering device development without physics research goals)

subspecialties.name: Biophysics
subspecialties.tagline: Physical approaches to biological systems and soft matter biophysics
covers: Single-molecule physics, biomechanics, active matter in biological contexts
Examples: motor-protein mechanics, physical models of cellular processes
explicitly_excludes: Biology / Life Sciences (biological studies without novel physical-method emphasis)

subspecialties.name: Soft Matter & Complex Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Polymers, colloids, active matter and complex-systems physics
covers: Rheology, self-assembly, active matter, network dynamics and emergent behavior
Examples: colloidal assembly experiments, models of flocking and network resilience

uncovered_concerns: Materials-heavy condensed-matter work: intentionally routed to Materials Science to avoid duplication; some borderline papers may need title/abstract heuristics.
Overlap between Quantum Information, AMO and Condensed Matter: these communities publish across boundaries; clear abstract-based routing rules will be required.

Instrumentation vs Engineering: distinguishing physics-motivated instrumentation from engineering development can be subjective; we keep an experimental-methods card but expect manual/ML disambiguation.
Astrophysics vs Cosmology boundary: observational cosmology (CMB, large-scale structure) can sit between the two; we separate by focus (cosmology = Universe-scale/early-Universe).
Biophysics is smaller here but some biological-physics work will go to Life Sciences if primarily biological rather than physics-driven.
